description: Fix dead-end UI elements and empty event handlers in quantsync index.tsx. The comment reply button has onClick={() => {}} which does nothing. Wire it to a proper action. Audit the page for any other dead handlers.
steps: In apps/quantsync/src/pages/index.tsx, the comment reply button (the one with emoji 'chat bubble') has onClick={() => {}}, which is an empty handler. Wire it to a real action - navigate to a post thread or open a reply dialog. Since there is no router, a reasonable approach is to call a handleReply(postId) function that sets state to show a reply input or calls window.location to navigate to /post/{id}.
Audit the same file for any href='#' patterns. If found, replace with proper navigation (e.g., post links should go to /post/{id}).
Check that all remaining onClick handlers in the file call actual functions (handleLike, handleRepost, handleBookmark all exist and do real work).
Run pnpm typecheck to verify changes compile.
Run pnpm build to verify build still passes.
acceptance_criteria: No onClick={() => {}} empty handlers remain in apps/quantsync/src/pages/index.tsx
No href='#' dead links remain in apps/quantsync/src/pages/index.tsx
The reply button calls a real function that either navigates or updates state
findings: No href='#' dead links were found in the file. The reply button was wired to handleReply (navigates to /post/{id}) and the share button was wired to handleShare (copies post URL to clipboard). Both typecheck and build pass cleanly.
